results.snippet: Deep dive into the ongoing CSSWG debate between grid-template-rows:masonry (extending CSS Grid) vs. a standalone display:masonry module. Covers browser-flag status as of mid-2025, progressive-enhancement patterns, and what each approach means for variable-height card grids in production today.
results.title: Masonry In CSS: Should Grid Evolve Or Stand Aside For A New Module? — Smashing Magazine

results.snippet: Official MDN reference for the masonry value in CSS Grid (grid-template-rows: masonry / grid-template-columns: masonry). Includes @supports progressive-enhancement patterns, current flag-gated browser support table, and code examples directly applicable to a multi-column mod-card browser.
results.title: Masonry layout — MDN Web Docs

results.snippet: Apple/WebKit's 2024 public RFC comparing the two competing masonry syntaxes with live demos. Explains why Safari Technology Preview ships grid-template-rows:masonry and what the final spec convergence timeline looks like — critical for deciding which syntax to target today.
results.title: Help us choose the final syntax for Masonry in CSS — WebKit Blog

results.snippet: Practical walkthrough of implementing masonry using only CSS Grid with the masonry row value plus a CSS columns fallback, with @supports guards. Directly addresses variable-height cards and the no-JS requirement — a close match to the 291-card mod browser use case.
results.title: Masonry with CSS Grid, finally a solution without JavaScript — utilitybend

results.snippet: 2026-current survey of card-grid layout patterns including bento grids, asymmetric featured-card layouts, and density toggles. Addresses the exact design vocabulary (bento, hero cards, density) mentioned in the original question for a mod/card browser UI.
results.title: CSS Layout Patterns in 2026: Grid, Flexbox, or Bento?

results.snippet: Step-by-step implementation of a fully CSS-only masonry layout using CSS columns as the baseline (works today, everywhere) with a CSS Grid masonry layer on top via @supports. The column-ordering trade-off (top-to-bottom within columns vs. left-to-right across rows) is explicitly discussed — relevant for deciding DOM order vs. visual order for 291 mod cards.
results.title: Building a responsive, progressively-enhanced masonry layout with only CSS and HTML
